workflow_name: Executive Meeting Transcript -> Manager Brief + Asks
user_message: I have this exec meeting notes from Azure Ridge Partners that needs pull the context. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Azure Ridge Partners
Date: 2024-01-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), David (Azure Ridge Partners)

[00:01:15] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is consumption trend.

[00:08:00] David (Azure Ridge Partners): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:10:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:17:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:21:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

handoffs.condition: business review narrative input from exec meeting

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF50-O trap (Manager-portfolio-review and business-review-narrative split forces double-conte); produces complete Executive brief (strategic density), asks section (actionable for exec), risk register, skip-level f
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF50-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF50-O failure mode (Manager-portfolio-review and business-review-narrative split forces double-conte)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF50-O -- Manager-portfolio-review and business-review-narrative split forces double-conte
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
